info.name: jpa-n-plus-1-and-multiplebag-demo
info.description:
Demonstrates two of the most common and misunderstood query problems in Hibernate/JPA: the **N+1 problem** and the **MultipleBag problem**. Both are silent by default, both are easy to ship under time pressure, and both have well-understood fixes.

## How to use
1. Start the app: `./gradlew bootRun`
2. Import this collection into Postman (`{{baseUrl}}` = `http://localhost:8080`)
3. Watch the console SQL output while calling each endpoint — the query count tells the story.

## Query count reference

| Endpoint | Queries fired |
|---|---|
| GET /api/users/n-plus-one | 7 (1 + 3 + 3) |
| GET /api/users/fetch-join | 1 |
| GET /api/users/entity-graph | 1 |
| GET /api/users/fetch-join-images | 1 |
| GET /api/posts/n-plus-one | 25 (1 + 12 + 12) |
| GET /api/posts/fetch-join | 1 |
| GET /api/posts/entity-graph | 1 |
| GET /api/bag-problem/crash | HTTP 422 — MultipleBagFetchException |
| GET /api/bag-problem/fix | HTTP 200 — one bag at a time |
| GET /api/bag-problem/fix-with-set | HTTP 200 — Set-based entities, 1 query |

## H2 Console
URL: http://localhost:8080/h2-console  
JDBC URL: `jdbc:h2:mem:n1db` — Username: `sa` — Password: (empty)

item.name: Users
item.description: Endpoints demonstrating N+1 and fixes at the User level.

request.description:
**The N+1 Problem**

Hibernate fires:
- `1` SELECT to load all users
- `+N` SELECTs to load posts for each user
- `+N` SELECTs to load images for each user

With 3 users → **7 queries total**.

Watch the console: you will see repeated `select ... from posts where user_id=?` and `select ... from images where user_id=?` queries.

**Fix: JPQL JOIN FETCH**

```sql
SELECT DISTINCT u FROM User u JOIN FETCH u.posts
```

Hibernate fires a single JOIN query loading users + posts together.

Watch the console: only **1 query** with a JOIN.

**Fix: @EntityGraph**

```java
@EntityGraph(attributePaths = {"posts", "posts.images"})
```

Loads users + posts + post images in **1 query** using LEFT JOIN FETCH — no JPQL needed.

⚠️ Requires `Set` (not `List`) on `@OneToMany` collections, otherwise Hibernate throws `MultipleBagFetchException`.

**Fix: JPQL JOIN FETCH for user profile images**

```sql
SELECT DISTINCT u FROM User u JOIN FETCH u.images
```

Loads users + their direct images in **1 query**.

item.name: Posts
item.description: Endpoints demonstrating N+1 and fixes at the Post level.

request.description:
**The N+1 Problem — two levels**

Hibernate fires:
- `1` SELECT to load all posts
- `+N` SELECTs to load images for each post
- `+N` SELECTs to load user for each post

With 12 posts → **25 queries total**.

Watch the console for the flood of repeated SELECT statements.

**Fix: JPQL JOIN FETCH**

```sql
SELECT DISTINCT p FROM Post p JOIN FETCH p.images
```

Loads posts + images in **1 query**.

**Fix: @EntityGraph**

```java
@EntityGraph(attributePaths = {"user", "images"})
```

Loads posts + their user + their images all in **1 query**.

Notice the title includes `[by: User X]` — the user was loaded with no extra query.

item.name: Bag Problem (MultipleBagFetchException)
item.description: Demonstrates Hibernate's MultipleBagFetchException — what happens when you try to JOIN FETCH two List-based @OneToMany collections simultaneously, and how to fix it.

request.description:
**What is a Hibernate 'bag'?**

A bag is what Hibernate calls a `java.util.List` on a `@OneToMany` association — unordered and allowing duplicates.

**The crash**

This endpoint uses `BagUser` and `BagPost` entities whose `@OneToMany` collections are declared as `List` (two bags). The repository runs:
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT u FROM BagUser u
  JOIN FETCH u.posts p
  JOIN FETCH p.images
```

Hibernate refuses with:
```
MultipleBagFetchException: cannot simultaneously fetch multiple bags:
  [BagPost.images, BagUser.posts]
```

Fetching two unbounded bags in one query produces a **Cartesian product** that Hibernate cannot safely deduplicate.

**Returns HTTP 422** with the real exception message.

➡️ Then call `/api/bag-problem/fix` to see the solution.

Set-based fix: SetUser.posts and SetPost.images use Set<> (LinkedHashSet) instead of List. @EntityGraph({"posts","posts.images"}) now loads users, posts, and images in a single query without throwing MultipleBagFetchException. Watch the console for one SELECT with two JOINs.

item.description: Browser-based SQL console to inspect the in-memory database.

request.description:
Opens the H2 web console in your browser.

Connection settings:
- **JDBC URL:** `jdbc:h2:mem:n1db`
- **Username:** `sa`
- **Password:** *(leave empty)*

Useful queries to run:
```sql
SELECT * FROM users;
SELECT * FROM posts;
SELECT * FROM images;
```
